question.text: A person goes short in GBPINR futures at Rs. 99.75 and at expiry the GBPINR FBIL reference rate is Rs. 100.75. What is the result?

answer.text: Loss of Re 1 per GBP (Rs. 1,000 per lot)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Short P&L = (Entry - FSP) × Lot = (99.75 - 100.75) × 1000 = -1.00 × 1000 = -Rs. 1,000 per lot (loss). The GBP appreciated, causing a loss on the short position. **The Trap:** Short position loses when price RISES above the entry price. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is directly from the NISM workbook sample questions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** An exporter who shorted GBPINR to hedge would offset this futures loss with the better spot conversion rate.
